title: Federal Reserve chair signals smaller rate hikes as bank keeps up inflation fight
summary: Financial markets have been anxiously awaiting signs about the bank’s plans. Powell put emphasis on the labor market, which has been transformed by the pandemic in ways economists still struggle to understand.

title: US gas prices plunge toward $3 a gallon as demand drops worldwide
summary: The price of gas is back to where it was before Russia invaded Ukraine and is heading toward $3 a gallon, defying earlier projections and offering an unexpected gift for the holidays.

title: New clinic planned in Alabama where enslaved women were tortured in medical experiments
summary: Michelle Browder has plans for a museum and clinic where 19th-century physician J. Marion Sims conducted cruel medical trials on Black women.

title: China vows crackdown on ‘hostile forces’ as demonstrations test President Xi
summary: A massive show of force by the security services Wednesday sought to deter further protests.

title: US economic growth better than forecast in third quarter
summary: Despite high interest rates and chronic inflation, the U.S. economy grew at a 2.9% annual rate from July through September, the government said Wednesday in a healthy upgrade from its initial estimate.

title: Eruption of Hawaii volcano draws spectators as it revives bad memories
summary: The first eruption in 38 years of Mauna Loa, the world’s largest active volcano, is attracting onlookers to a national park for “spectacular” views.

title: How a bipartisan group of senators got same-sex marriage protections passed
summary: The group spent months working to get 12 Republicans - including both Alaska senators - onboard with the legislation.

title: Anchorage Assembly directs $400,000 to help relieve safety issues surrounding Sullivan Arena homeless shelter
summary: The funding will help set up security and trash cleaning services at three city properties near the shelter. The president of Fairview Community Council called it“a tourniquet to stop the bleeding.”

title: Embattled North Star hospital CEO stepping down, ‘effective immediately’
summary: The departure of the Anchorage hospital’s top executive comes as North Star faces scrutiny from regulators, and a week after the Daily News published an account of deepening dysfunction at the psychiatric hospital for children.

title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes found guilty of seditious conspiracy in Jan. 6 case
summary: One of Rhodes’ co-defendants — Kelly Meggs, the leader of the Florida chapter of the Oath Keepers — was also convicted of the sedition charge, while three other co-defendants were cleared of the charge.

title: Justice Department intervenes for Mississippi capital’s struggling water system
summary: The department filed a proposal to appoint a third-party manager for the Jackson water system, which nearly collapsed earlier this year.

title: Five Aleutian volcanoes are now showing signs of unrest
summary: The latest is the ice-filled caldera of Takawangha, which rises 4,753 feet out of the Bering Sea in Alaska’s far western Aleutian Islands.

title: Houston lifts boil-water order affecting more than 2 million area residents
summary: The city said water quality samples sent to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality confirmed the tap water meets regulatory standards and is safe to drink.

title: NATO chief commits to future Ukraine membership, reinforces need for continued military aid
summary: Many of NATO’s 30 allies believe the focus now must solely be on defeating Russia, and NATO Secretary-General Jens Stoltenberg stressed that any immediate attempt to move ahead on membership could divide them.

title: Students sent home, police on patrol as China curbs protests over COVID lockdowns
summary: Authorities have eased some controls after demonstrations in at least eight mainland cities and Hong Kong — but showed no sign of backing off their larger “zero-COVID” strategy.

title: Santa’s back in town, with inflation and inclusion on his mind

title: Study: Incentives can lead employees to cheat or lie at work
summary: Rewards, bonuses, and incentives are used heavily in American workplaces to achieve performance targets, but this focus can lead to ethical lapses, research shows.

title: Thanksgiving travel was not a fiasco. Is that a good sign for Christmas?
summary: Airlines staffed up for Thanksgiving, and flight cancellations were low for the holiday, even if storms led to Sunday delays.

title: Anchorage man charged in mobile home park shooting was involved in SWAT standoff days later

title: Biden calls on Congress to intervene in rail dispute, head off potential strike

title: Ukraine’s drone hunters scramble to destroy Russia’s Iranian-built fleet
summary: Russia has retreated on the battlefield but continues to strike Ukraine with Iranian-made Shahed drones., so Ukraine’s drone hunters patrol at night to shoot them down.

title: Sen. Murkowski pushes for a half-billion dollars in earmarks for Alaska
summary: After a 10-year hiatus, congressionally directed spending returned to Capitol Hill in 2021. Sen. Lisa Murkowski is advocating for 130 earmarked projects across the state, while Sen. Dan Sullivan has a different philosophy on earmarks.

title: After canceling sale earlier this year, Biden administration to hold Cook Inlet oil and gas lease sale in December
summary: The sale was required in this year’s Inflation Reduction Act. An industry group said it could lead to oil and natural gas the world still needs. A conservation group said it will threaten wildlife and the climate.

summary: The world’s largest active volcano was erupting Monday for the first time since 1984 but wasn’t immediately threatening communities.

summary: David Wayne Anderson, 49, faces charges including attempted murder, kidnapping and sexual assault.

title: Death in study of CRISPR gene therapy sparks search for answers
summary: Terry Horgan, the lone volunteer in a unique study involving a gene-editing technique, has died, and those behind the trial are now trying to figure out what killed him.

title: Clock runs out on efforts to make daylight saving time permanent
summary: A bill to permanently “spring forward” has been stalled in Congress for more than seven months.

title: ADN Politics podcast: What we’re watching before Alaska’s polls close
summary: In this episode: What can we learn from last-minute campaign moves? When can Alaskans expect to see who won? How is voter turnout looking so far? Do we expect any candidates to challenge the results?

title: Judge says he’ll appoint outside monitor for Donald Trump’s company
summary: A Manhattan judge ordered an outside watchdog as he presides over a lawsuit in which New York’s attorney general alleges Trump and the Trump Organization misled banks and others about the value of prized assets, including golf courses and hotels bearing his name.

title: Exploding air bags spur do-not-drive advisory for older Dodge and Chrysler vehicles
summary: The recall applies to Dodge Magnums, Chargers and Challengers, as well as Chrysler 300s. The affected model years are 2005 to 2010.

title: Angst over youth outmigration emerges in Alaska campaign rhetoric and debates
summary: University funding, career prospects and lifestyle amenities are factors cited as important to young Alaskans pondering their futures

title: A TikToker hit a golf ball into the Grand Canyon. She now faces charges.
summary: A TikToker hit a golf ball into the Grand Canyon. She now faces charges.

title: 2 alleged ‘boogaloo’ members arrested in Michigan and Ohio
summary: The FBI has arrested two alleged members of the far-rightanti-government group the Boogaloo Boys, with authorities increasinglyconcerned about the potential for violence in the leadup to next week’smidterm elections.

title: Alaska’s top election official confident in state’s voting systems, but expresses concerns about election misinformation
summary: Lt. Gov. Kevin Meyer, who oversees elections, said he’s satisfied that Alaska’s ballot-counting processes in the Nov 8 general election will work to keep voting secure. But he said misinformation about voter fraud is a concern.

title: Anchorage police called to North Star hospital 3 times in one night after young patients break glass, activate sprinklers
summary: The rapid-fire police responses were the latest in what has been a string of trouble at the private psychiatric hospital for kids and adolescents.

title: Anchorage School District recommends program cuts and consolidations, plus school closure repurpose plans to help with budget woes
summary: Officials have already recommended the closure of six district elementary schools as a way to save costs but say they need to make various changes and cuts to make up for a nearly $68 million deficit.

title: Police investigating ‘false’ reports of high school shootings in Anchorage and Fairbanks
summary: Two calls within about 30 minutes reported active shooters at Eagle River and Lathrop high schools Wednesday afternoon, police say. Police said the calls were determined to be a hoax.

title: Multi-vehicle accident with injuries diverting traffic on southbound Seward Highway
summary: Police were directing southbound Seward Highway traffic to exit at Dowling Road Wednesday afternoon.

title: Glaciers in Yosemite and Africa will disappear by 2050, U.N. warns
summary: Even if global warming is limited to just 1.5 degrees Celsius, which now seems unlikely, all glaciers in Yosemite National Park and in Africa will be lost.

title: Paul Pelosi is still in the hospital. Here’s what’s known about his injuries.
summary: Few details have emerged about Paul Pelosi’s treatment, his current condition or his prognosis almost a week after an assailant broke into their house and attacked him with a hammer.

title: Storm brings little snow to Anchorage but up to 2 feet in western Kenai Peninsula and Susitna Valley
summary: The storm brought a band of heavy precipitation to areas west of the city, forecasters say. The storm is expected to taper off Thursday.

title: University of Alaska administration and faculty union reach tentative contract agreement
summary: While a 14-month negotiation has ended, unfair labor practice complaints process are still ongoing.

title: Constitutional convention supporters target Alaska’s judicial selection process
summary: Supporters of Alaska’s current system say it’s the “gold standard,” while conservative critics think it’s time for a change.

title: Musk vows no quick return for suspended accounts, angering some supporters
summary: Elon Musk pledged Wednesday after a Zoom meeting with civil rights groups that Twitter will not reinstate banned accounts until it has a clear process to do so.

title: Chinese workers flee world’s biggest iPhone plant over virus restrictions
summary: Foxconn is struggling to contain an exodus of workers at the plant making half the world’s iPhones in the latest example of the economic and social costs China’s zero COVID policy.

title: Republicans to probe ‘woke capitalism’ of climate-focused investing after midterms
summary: GOP lawmakers plan to conduct oversight of Wall Street’s policies on ESG. Firms say it makes money for investors and reduces risk.

title: Russia rejoins key wartime deal on Ukrainian grain exports
summary: Diplomatic efforts salvaged an agreement that allowed Ukrainian grain and other commodities to reach world markets, after Ukraine pledged not to use a designated Black Sea corridor to attack Russian forces.

title: Federal observers flagged issues with Native language support in Alaska’s August election
summary: Observers in August found some polling places lacked election workers trained to assist Yup’ik-speaking voters, which could violate the federal Voting Rights Act.

title: Western Alaska village school shut down after principal banished and teachers flown out
summary: The principal and several school staff members left the community of Kipnuk in Southwest Alaska over the weekend in two chartered planes following reports of a banishment order, occupation of a school building and a brief blockade during a visit by Alaska State Troopers.

title: Federal observers flagged issues with Native language support in Alaska’s August election
summary: Observers in August found some polling places lacked election workers trained to assist Yup’ik-speaking voters, which could violate the federal Voting Rights Act.

title: Capitol Police cameras caught break-in at Pelosi home, but no one was watching
summary: The attack on Paul Pelosi last week has sparked an urgent conversation about lawmaker security amid increased threats.

title: U.S. officials exploring whether they have legal authority to review foreign investments in Elon Musk’s purchase of Twitter
summary: Terms of the deal give large foreign investors access to confidential information about the social media platform.

title: New coronavirus subvariants make gains as omicron strain BA.5 fades
summary: What this ultimately means for this fall and winter — a period when many health experts have already predicted some degree of COVID-19 resurgence — remains to be seen.

title: China fishing fleet defied the U.S. in confrontation on the high seas
summary: Nobody was hurt in the confrontation, but it represented a breach of international maritime protocol that has inflamed tensions between the two nations.

title: Cyclist killed in collision with car on Seward Highway ramp in Midtown Anchorage
summary: The male cyclist was northbound on a ramp to Tudor Road when a Subaru Legacy collided with him, police said.

title: ‘Chaos and confusion’: The campaign to stamp out ballot drop boxes
summary: Ballot drop boxes have become a symbol of the attacks on voter access. Campaigns against them are increasingly succeeding. Laws, court rulings and local and state politicians in at least a dozen states have targeted drop boxes in the last two years.

title: 2 to 10 inches of snow forecast for Southcentral Alaska through Wednesday
summary: Anchorage could get up to 4 inches of snow from a storm expected to arrive first on the Kenai Peninsula, though the forecast remains uncertain.

title: Paul Pelosi, 82, faces ‘long recovery’ after hammer attack, says House speaker’s office
summary: In a voluntary interview with San Francisco police after his arrest, suspect David DePape said he set out to hold Nancy Pelosi hostage and interrogate and torture her, according to the federal charges.

title: Senate candidate Mehmet Oz’s medical research was rejected in 2003, resulting in 2-year ban
summary: At issue were questions about the strength of the data used by Oz to reach an important medical conclusion, according to people who recalled the events.

title: My girlfriend struggles with seasonal depression and I’m already dreading her dread. What can I do to help?
summary: Both Wayne and Wanda have a few divergent suggestions on how changing up a routine could change a dour outlook on winter.

title: Positive data on RSV vaccine lifts hopes for newborns after years of futility
summary: The common but scary respiratory virus fills U.S. hospitals with wheezing babies each fall.

title: Netanyahu looms over Israel’s fifth election in four years
summary: Israeli-Palestinian tensions are boiling over and Iran remains a central threat, the foremost issue in the vote once again is former leader Benjamin Netanyahu and his fitness to serve amid corruption charges.

title: Anchorage man charged with arson in apartment complex fire that destroyed five units
summary: The fire Sunday, which prompted evacuations, was started by a 23-year-old man angry after neighbors refused to give him cigarettes, according to charges filed Monday.

title: Company to pay $1M for actions related to burning waste oil and jet fuel at Prince William Sound salmon hatchery
summary: The Prince William Sound Aquaculture Corp. has struggled to deal with waste oil and fuel at some of the remote hatcheries it operates, according to the plea agreement.

title: Biden administration relaxes rules for student debt forgiveness programs
summary: The new rules aiming to make it easier for borrowers to get relief if they are duped by their colleges or if they put in a decade of work as public servants take hold in July, and are separate from President Joe Biden’s sweeping student debt forgiveness plan.
